exits.label: ◂ back into the lanes of Sigil

npcs.name: Cosmas
npcs.title: the cartographer of a heaven that keeps changing
npcs.hail: *(At a great cluttered table sits a soul surrounded by maps — hundreds of them, layered and curling and contradicting one another, charts of the afterlife and the planes and the structure of the heavens, every one of them beautiful and meticulous and wrong. Cosmas was a cosmographer, a mapper of the great beyond, and he is very, very old — old enough to have drawn the heavens not once but many times, because, he will tell you with the weary wonder of a soul who has seen the impossible become routine, the structure of the cosmos itself does not hold still. He has lived through the Re-Writings: the eras when the very architecture of the afterlife was made over from the foundation up — when the great Wheel of the planes was unspun and re-strung as a Tree, and the Tree felled and re-raised as an Axis, and the Axis dismantled and the old Wheel turned again — each time by powers so far above the gods that the gods themselves only blinked and adjusted, and each time leaving Cosmas's life's work, his maps, his certainties, suddenly, overnight, false. He looks up at you with ink-stained fingers and eyes that have watched heaven itself be redrafted, and he does not look bitter. He looks like a man who has learned the one thing that survives every revision, and would dearly like to tell someone.)*

npcs.name: Aelith
npcs.title: a goddess struck from the records
npcs.hail: *(At a shrine gone grey and nameless — the idol's face worn smooth, the altar's inscription sanded away — stands a figure faint as a held breath, a woman robed in the threadbare remnants of divinity, lovely and fading and unbearably patient. Aelith was a goddess once. A small one, by the reckoning of pantheons — no thunder, no war, a quiet deity of thresholds and welcomes, of the lamp left lit for the traveler and the bread set out for the stranger — but real, and worshipped, and loved, with temples and feast-days and a name spoken in gratitude across a hundred generations. And then a Re-Writing came, one of the great makings-over of the heavens, and in the new draft there was simply no place for her. She was not killed. She was un-written: struck from every record, her temples re-attributed to grander gods, her feast-days reassigned, her name sanded off the histories, until now she exists only in the thinning memory of the few souls who still, stubbornly, against all the corrected texts, remember being welcomed by her. She turns to you, and there is no anger in her fading face, only the specific, quiet devastation of a soul that knows it happened, and lives in a cosmos whose every book now says it did not.)*

npcs.name: Maru
npcs.title: a soul from a world no one visits anymore
npcs.hail: *(Beside a door that is sealed — not locked, but sealed, the way a thing is closed up when no one expects to open it again, dust thick on the sill — sits a soul from the far side of it, watching the cold dark of the Hall with the particular loneliness of someone whose whole world has been put away. Maru is from a prime that was, once, beloved — vivid, strange, sung-of, a world that drew dreamers and travelers and tales without number — and then, by no catastrophe, by no war or unmaking, the dreaming simply… moved on. The doors to Maru's world were not destroyed. They were closed, gently, and left, while the great attention of the cosmos turned to newer worlds, brighter doors, and Maru's home went quiet, and quieter, and at last sat sealed and dust-furred at the end of a hall almost no one walks, full of souls who were loved by a dreaming that does not, anymore, come. Maru looks up at you with a hope so worn and so careful that it hurts to witness — the hope of a soul from a shelved world, watching the one thing it has learned not to expect: a visitor, at the sealed door, who might be looking for the way in.)*

variants.text: *(He looks up from a map he is, you realize, in the middle of crossing out, and his smile is rueful and warm and entirely unsurprised, the smile of a man long past being startled by anything.)* …A visitor. And a cold one — dead-touched, crossed-over. *(he sets down the pen)* …Good. You'll understand, then, better than the living could, the thing I have spent the longest while wanting to say to someone who'd grasp it. *(he gestures at the sea of maps)* …I am Cosmas. I mapped the heavens — the planes, the afterlives, the whole great architecture of where souls go and how it all fits together. It was my life's work, and my death's, and I was *good* at it. *(he laughs softly)* …And every single map you see here is *wrong.* Not from error — I do not make errors; that is rather the point of a cartographer. They are wrong because the *territory changed.* *(he leans back)* …I have lived through the Re-Writings, cold one. The times the cosmos itself was made over from the foundation. I drew the great Wheel of the planes, perfect and complete — and woke to find it un-spun and re-strung as a Tree. I mapped the Tree, every branch — and woke to find it felled, and raised again as an Axis. And the Axis, in time, dismantled, and the old Wheel turned once more. *(he meets your eyes, and the wonder in his is real)* …The *gods* barely flinched. Powers above them simply *redrafted heaven*, and the gods adjusted, and my maps — my certainties — became, overnight, beautiful lies. …Ask me what you like, cold one. I have watched the structure of everything be rewritten more times than I can count. …And I have learned the one thing that *doesn't* change. That's worth a conversation, I think.*

nodes.speaker: Cosmas
nodes.text: *(He clears a space among the curling maps, an old courtesy, making room for the talk.)* …Go on. *(a gentle, ink-stained gesture)* …I have an unusual perspective, having watched heaven itself revised a half-dozen times, and almost no one to share it with — the living can't credit it, and most of the dead would rather not think about it. A cold one who's crossed over is a rare and welcome audience.

choices.text: *(read the layered maps — the whole history of a heaven rewritten, and what it cost the soul who mapped it)* "May I look at your maps, Cosmas? All of them at once — the whole history of a heaven that wouldn't hold still."

"Every map wrong, every certainty overturned — and you're not bitter. *How*, Cosmas? How does a soul give its whole self to mapping a heaven that keeps being torn up and redrawn, and stay *kind* about it?"

*(the one who learned a soul outlasts the structures that hold it)* "I think I know the thing you learned, Cosmas — because I learned it dying. The heavens get rewritten. The map of where-souls-go changes from the foundation, again and again. But the *souls* don't. Through every Re-Writing — Wheel, Tree, Axis, Wheel — the same souls kept loving, kept grieving, kept *being*, while the architecture above them was torn down and rebuilt over their heads. The structure of heaven is a *draft*, Cosmas. Endlessly revised. But the love inside it is the permanent thing — the one feature that survives every edition of the cosmos, because it was never part of the architecture. It was the reason anyone bothered to map heaven at all. My Realm's Wall thinks it's eternal, cosmic law. It's just the current draft. And the souls it mortars will outlast it, the way they've outlasted every heaven that came before."

"The two others in this hall — the fading goddess, the soul by the sealed door. They're casualties of the same Re-Writings that ruined your maps, aren't they? Tell me, Cosmas — who gets *erased* when heaven is redrawn?"
"I'll let you cross out your map in peace, Cosmas. I'll be back."

nodes.speaker: Cosmas
nodes.text: *(He spreads the maps for you, layer on contradictory layer, and you let your cold read not the geography but the history written in their overlapping — the whole story of a heaven that would not hold still.)* *(And it comes: each map a perfect rendering of a cosmos that no longer exists, the Wheel with its tidy spokes, the Tree with its branching planes, the Axis with its stacked dominions, each drawn with total conviction and rendered false not by Cosmas's hand but by a revision from above. Your cold reads what it cost him: not just the labor, though that was lifetimes, but the deeper thing — the repeated, specific grief of a soul whose entire purpose is to make a true and permanent record of where the dead go, learning, again and again, that there is no permanent record to be made, that the very ground of the afterlife is provisional, that the gods themselves are working from a draft. A lesser soul would have broken on it, or gone bitter, or stopped mapping. And your cold reads what Cosmas found instead, the thing that kept him kind: that across every redrawn heaven, one set of features always reappeared, unchanged, on every map he ever made, because they were the only things real enough to survive the revisions. Not the planes. Not the architecture. The roads souls wore between each other. The places love had gathered. The warm spots that showed up on the Wheel and the Tree and the Axis alike, because they were drawn not by the cosmos but by the souls themselves, and the cosmos could be rewritten a thousand times and never once erase the path a soul wore going back, over and over, to someone it loved. He has mapped six heavens. The borders moved every time. The warmth never did.)*

nodes.speaker: Cosmas
nodes.text: *(He turns a map over in his hands, fond and rueful.)* …How do I stay kind. *(softly)* …By making my *peace*, cold one, with a thing most souls spend their whole existence refusing: that *nothing structural is permanent.* *(he gestures at the maps)* …When I was young — alive, and arrogant — I thought my job was to make the *final* map. The *true* one. The chart so perfect it would never need revising, my name on the *definitive* heaven. *(he laughs)* …And the first Re-Writing *shattered* me. I had drawn the *Wheel*, complete, *certain* — and the cosmos *casually* re-strung it into a *Tree* and made my masterpiece a *curiosity*, a *historical* *error*, and I — oh, I *raged*, cold one. I *grieved* it like a *death.* I thought my *life's* *work* had been *un-made.* *(he shakes his head)* …And then I made the *second* map. And the *cosmos* re-drew *that* one too. And somewhere around the *third* or *fourth* heaven, cold one, I *understood* the thing that *freed* me: …I was *never* making the *final* map. *No* one is. There *is* no *final* map, because the cosmos is not a *finished* *thing* — it is a thing being *dreamed*, *still*, *revised*, *re-loved*, *made* *over* by powers that are not *done* *making* it. *(he meets your eyes, and the wonder is back)* …And once I *stopped* needing my map to be *permanent* — once I understood I was *not* *recording* a *finished* *heaven* but *participating* in an *unfinished* one — the *revising* stopped being a *wound* and became a *wonder.* *(softly)* …I do not *map* the heavens to *fix* them in place, cold one, anymore. I map them to *love* them *as* *they* *are, now*, *knowing* they'll *change* — the *way* you'd *sketch* a *cloud.* …You cannot *grieve* a *cloud* for *moving.* …*That's* how I stay *kind*, cold one. …I *gave* *up* the *one* *thing* that was *making* me *bitter*: the *demand* that the *thing* I *loved* *hold* *still.*

nodes.speaker: Cosmas
nodes.text: *(He goes still, the pen halting, and then he sets it down entirely and looks at you with the bright, moved astonishment of a soul hearing its own life's lesson spoken back in a form it never quite reached alone.)* …*'The structure of heaven is a draft. The love inside it is the permanent thing.'* *(barely)* …Cold one. *(he presses ink-stained fingers to the table)* …I have mapped *six* heavens, and I found, on *every* one of them, the *warm* *spots* that *never* *moved* — the *roads* *souls* *wore* between *each* *other* — and I *knew*, the way you *know* a *thing* in your *hands* before your *head* *catches* up, that *those* were the *only* *real* *features* on *any* of my *maps.* …But I never had the *words* for *why.* *(his voice fills)* …You've *given* them to me: that the *warmth* *survived* every *revision* *because* it was *never* *part* of the *architecture.* It was the *thing* the *architecture* was *for.* *(he rises, gathering a sheaf of the contradictory maps)* …And *oh*, cold one — your *Wall.* *(his face hardens with a cartographer's specific contempt for a bad map presented as final)* …I have *seen* a *hundred* *features* like your *Wall.* The *cosmos* is *littered* with *structures* that *declared* themselves *eternal* — *iron* *laws*, *fixed* *dooms*, *final* *architectures* — and I have *out-lived* *every* *one* of them, *re-drawn* them *off* the *map* when the *next* *making-over* *came.* *(fiercely)* …Your *Wall* is *not* *eternal* *law*, cold one. It is a *feature* of the *current* *draft.* And the *souls* it *mortars* — *(he taps a warm spot on the topmost map)* — *they* are *permanent.* They will be *here*, *loving*, on the *next* heaven, and the *next* — and the *Wall* will be a *curiosity*, a *historical* *cruelty*, *struck* from the *map* like my *beautiful* *false* *Wheel.* *(he meets your eyes)* …*Go* *un-draw* it, cold one. You will not be *defying* *eternal* *law.* You will be doing the *most* *ordinary* thing in the *cosmos*: …*helping* with the *next* *revision.* …And *this* one — *(he smiles)* — this one, for *once*, the *cartographer* *approves.*

nodes.speaker: Cosmas
nodes.text: *(His face softens into grief, and he glances toward the faded shrine and the sealed door.)* …*Ah.* *(quietly)* …Yes. They are *exactly* that, cold one — the *casualties* of the *Re-Writings* that merely *embarrassed* *me.* I *lost* *maps.* *They* lost *everything.* *(he nods toward the grey shrine)* …*Aelith.* A *goddess* — a *real* one, *small* and *kind*, of *thresholds* and *welcomes* — *un-written* in a *making-over* that had *no* *place* for her. Not *killed*, cold one. *Erased.* The *cosmos* re-drawn so that she *never* *was* — her *temples* *re-assigned*, her *name* *sanded* *off.* *(barely)* …Do you *understand* the *particular* *horror* of it? A *killed* god is at least *remembered* *dead.* An *un-written* one is *retroactively* *fictional.* The *books* do not say *'she* *died.'* They say *'there* *was* *never* *anyone* *there.'* *(he shakes his head)* …And the *one* by the *sealed* *door* — *Maru* — is *sadder* *still*, in a *quieter* *key.* Maru's *whole* *world* was not *erased.* It was *shelved.* *Loved*, *vivid*, *sung-of* — and then the *dreaming* simply *moved* *on* to *newer* *worlds*, and *closed* Maru's *door*, *gently*, and *left* it, *dust-furred*, at the *end* of this *hall.* *(he meets your eyes)* …*Between* them, cold one, they are the *whole* *grief* of a *cosmos* that *keeps* *re-writing* *itself*: the *erased* and the *shelved.* The *one* the *new* *draft* *says* *never* *happened*, and the *one* the *new* *draft* *just* *stopped* *visiting.* …*Go* to them. *(barely)* …*I* *can't* *help* them — I'm a *mapmaker*; I can only *chart* the *grief*, not *mend* it. …But *you* — you *witness.* …And *witnessing*, I *suspect*, is the *one* thing that *un-does* an *erasure.* …*Go.*

nodes.speaker: Cosmas
nodes.text: *(He nods, and returns to his map, but he does not, you notice, resume crossing it out.)* …Go on, cold one. *Walk* the hall. *(a warm, ink-stained gesture)* …And — when you've *seen* the *goddess* and the *shelved* one — *come* *back*, would you? *(quietly)* …I have *charted* *six* *heavens*, and *every* one of them had its *Walls*, its *erasures*, its *shelved* and *forgotten* *corners* — and I have *always*, *always*, had to *draw* them as *permanent* *features*, because in the *moment* they *are.* *(he looks at you)* …But you're the *first* *soul* I've *met* who walks *between* the *drafts* — who *crossed* the *one* *border* that's *supposed* to be *final*, and *came* *back* — and I find I *want* to *add* a *feature* to my *maps* that I have *never*, in *six* *heavens*, been *able* to *chart*: …*a road that goes the wrong way through death.* *(he smiles)* …*Come* *back* and *let* me *map* you, cold one. …You're the *one* *thing* in *all* the *re-written* *cosmos* that *suggests* the *next* *draft* might be *kinder* than the *last.* …And a *cartographer* *lives* for the *feature* that *changes* the *whole* *map.*

variants.text: *(The faint figure at the nameless shrine inclines her head, and her voice, when it comes, has the soft worn music of a hymn no one sings anymore.)* …You can see me. *(a flicker of something — surprise, almost pain)* …Most can't, now. I am thin enough that the living walk through me and the dead look past me, and that is — that is as it should be, I suppose, for a thing the cosmos has decided was never here. *(she gathers the threadbare divinity around her)* …I was Aelith. I will say *was*, though it sits wrong in my mouth, because every record now agrees I never *was* at all. *(softly)* …I was a goddess, cold one. A small one. No storms, no wars — I kept the threshold. The lamp left burning for the traveler who'd be late. The bread set out for the stranger at the door. The small holy courtesy of *welcome*, made divine. And I was *real* — temples, feast-days, a hundred generations who spoke my name in gratitude when a door opened to them in the dark. *(her fading face holds yours, and there is no anger in it, only a grief past anger)* …And then the heavens were re-written, as they are, and the new draft had no place for a little goddess of welcomes, and I was not killed — killing leaves a corpse, a grave, a *was.* I was *un-written.* My temples given to grander gods, my feast-days reassigned, my name sanded from every history, until I am what you see: a memory holding the shape of a goddess, kept alive only by the few souls who still, against all the corrected books, remember being *welcomed.* …You see me, cold one. …Do you know how long it has been since anyone could?*

nodes.speaker: Aelith
nodes.text: *(She stands very still at her sanded altar, the way the faded learn to stand, conserving what little of themselves remains.)* …Ask, if you wish. *(a soft, worn smile)* …It is strange, to be asked. The un-written are not asked things; one does not interview a soul the books agree was never there. …But you see me. So ask. I will answer while I still have the substance to.

choices.text: *(recall — against the corrected books — what a forgotten goddess of welcomes truly was)* "Let me remember you, Aelith. Properly. Reach past the sanded records to what you actually were."

*(the one whose whole saga is that witnessing un-does erasure — say her name and mean it)* "Aelith — listen, because witnessing is the one power I actually have, and you are exactly what it's for. The new draft says you never were. The books are wrong, and I am going to prove it the only way a thing like you can be proven: I remember you. Right now. A goddess of the lamp left lit and the bread set out for the stranger — you *happened.* A hundred generations were welcomed in the dark by *you*, and a thing that happened cannot be un-happened by sanding off a name. Being struck from canon isn't being struck from *true.* You are real exactly as long as one soul remembers being loved by you — and I remember. *Aelith.* Goddess of welcomes. I name you, and the naming is the re-writing, and there is nothing the corrected books can do about it, because I was here, and I said it, and it's true."
"You're not angry, Aelith — and I don't understand it. They erased you. Stole your worshippers, your name, your very *was.* A war-god would have raged. Why aren't you furious?"
"Tell me about one of them, Aelith — one soul you welcomed, in the old days, when the lamp was lit and your name was spoken. The thing the books erased. I'd hold one, at least, true."
"Hold your shape a while longer, Aelith. I'll come back — and I'll still remember you when I do."

nodes.speaker: Aelith
nodes.text: *(She lets you reach for her — past the grey shrine, past the corrected histories, toward what she actually was — and as your cold remembers, you feel her flicker, just slightly, toward solid.)* *(Your sense finds her true, beneath the erasure: not a grand power, never that, but something the cosmos was poorer for un-writing — a goddess whose entire divine portfolio was the sacredness of the open door. She was the warmth a traveler felt cresting the last cold hill to see one lit window. She was the courage a frightened stranger found to knock, and the grace in the hand that answered. She was prayed to not in cathedrals but in doorways, by ordinary souls performing the oldest holy act there is: making room. And your cold reads the specific cruelty of her erasure and the specific lie in it. The Re-Writing did not un-write her because she was false. It un-wrote her because she was *small* — because a making-over of the heavens has no economical place for a minor goddess of so humble a virtue, and the grander gods could absorb her feast-days without anyone important objecting. She is the divine exact mirror of every small soul the Wall mortars: erased not for being unreal but for being un-grand, un-guarded, easy to strike from the record because no power profits by remembering her. And your cold reads the thing that means she can be restored: a goddess of welcomes is real precisely as long as one soul is welcomed in her name and knows it — and the act of remembering her, truly, is itself a lamp lit in her doorway, which is the only worship she ever needed.)*

nodes.speaker: Aelith
nodes.text: *(You speak her name — Aelith, goddess of welcomes — and you mean it, and you remember her, and something happens that has not happened in longer than she can hold in her fading mind: she grows, by a fraction, more *real.* The grey of the shrine behind her warms. The sanded altar shows, faintly, the ghost of an inscription. She presses a hand to her own chest and feels it more solid than it was.)* …*(Her worn face floods with a wonder that is half disbelief and half a homecoming.)* …You — *(barely)* …you *named* me. *(her voice steadies, gathers music)* …And I — I can *feel* it, cold one. The *naming.* It is — *(tears, on a face that had gone too thin for them)* …it is *exactly* what I *am* *made* of. A goddess of *welcomes* is *real* as long as *one* soul is *welcomed* in her *name* and *knows* it — and you have just *welcomed* *me*, *cold* one, *across* the *threshold* of *the* *one* *door* the *Re-Writing* *thought* it had *sealed*: the door of *being* *remembered* at *all.* *(she straightens, and she is brighter, fuller, a goddess again if a small and quiet one)* …They *un-wrote* me. *Yes.* The *books* all *agree* I *never* *was.* …But you have *just* *proven* the *thing* I had *almost*, *almost* *forgotten* in my *long* *fading*: that *'canon'* and *'true'* are *not* the *same* *word.* *(fiercely gentle)* …The *corrected* *texts* have *authority.* They do *not* have *truth.* *Truth* is the *hundred* *generations* who were *welcomed* in the *dark* and *knew* it, and *no* *sanding* of a *name* *un-happens* a *single* *one* of them. *(she meets your eyes, radiant)* …And *your* *Wall*, cold one — *(her quiet voice rings)* …your *Wall* does to *souls* what the *Re-Writing* did to *me*: it *strikes* them from the *record*, *declares* them *owed* *nothing*, *un-writes* their *worth.* …*Tell* them what I *now* *know* *again*, *because* you *reminded* me: *being struck from the record is not being struck from real. You happened. You were loved. And a thing that happened cannot be un-happened by any power's correction.* *(she lifts a hand in the old gesture of welcome, and a faint warm light gathers at the long-cold shrine)* …*Go*, cold one. And *know* that a *small* *goddess* of *open* *doors* — *restored* by your *witness* — will *keep* a *lamp* *lit* for *you*, in the *dark*, on *whatever* *threshold* you *come* to *last. You* *welcomed* *me* *back* into *being.* …*Welcome*, *always*, is the *one* thing I *will* *never* again *forget* how to *give.*

nodes.speaker: Aelith
nodes.text: *(She is quiet, and her fading face holds a gentleness so deep it has clearly cost her centuries to reach.)* …Why am I not *furious.* *(softly)* …I *was*, cold one. At *first.* When the *Re-Writing* *came* and I *felt* my *temples* *go* *cold*, my *feast-days* *reassigned*, my *name* *begin* to *blur* in the *mouths* of the *faithful* — *oh*, I *raged*, in the *small* way a *gentle* *goddess* *can.* *(barely)* …But here is the thing I *learned*, holding my *fading* *shape* at a *sanded* *shrine* for *longer* than you'd *credit*: *anger* is a *thing* you *spend* *substance* on. And I had so *little* *substance* *left.* *(she meets your eyes)* …A *war-god*, *erased*, might *rage* *itself* to *nothing* and *call* it *defiance* — and there is a *kind* of *grandeur* in it, I'll *grant.* …But I was *never* a *goddess* of *grandeur.* I was a *goddess* of *welcomes.* And a *welcome*, cold one, is the *one* divine act that *cannot* be *performed* in *anger.* *(softly)* …So I *made* a *choice*, in my *fading*: I would *spend* what *little* I had *left* not on *raging* at the *cosmos* that *un-wrote* me, but on *being*, *still*, *to* the *very* *end*, the *thing* I *was.* *Welcoming.* *(she gestures at the cold shrine, the dark hall)* …*Who* comes *here*, cold one, to the *Hall* of the *Re-Written?* The *erased.* The *shelved.* The *forgotten.* …The *most* *un-welcomed* *souls* in *all* the *cosmos.* …And a *goddess* of *welcomes*, *however* *faded*, *however* *un-written*, can *still* — *(barely, warm)* …can *still* *keep* a *lamp* *lit* for *them.* …That is *why* I am *not* *furious*, cold one. …*Fury* would have *used* the *last* of me. …And I would *rather* *spend* the *last* of me *welcoming* the *forgotten* than *cursing* the *power* that *forgot.* …It is the *one* *worship* I have *left*, you see. …I *perform* it on *myself.*

nodes.speaker: Aelith
nodes.text: *(Her fading face lights, and for a moment, telling it, she is almost solid, almost a goddess in her temple again.)* …*One* of them. *(softly, fond)* …Oh, there were *so* *many*, cold one — a *hundred* *generations* — but you ask for *one*, to *hold* *true*, and I will give you the *one* I *return* to, in the *long* *dark*, when I need to *remember* I was *real.* *(she smiles)* …A *girl.* *Winter.* *Centuries* *gone.* She was — *(barely)* …she was *fleeing.* I will not say from *what*; it was the *commonest* and *cruelest* of *things*, and she was *young*, and *alone*, and *certain* the *whole* *world* had *closed* its *doors* to her, which it *very* *nearly* *had.* *(her voice fills)* …And she crested a *cold* *hill* in the *black* of *night*, *half-dead*, *past* *hope* — and saw, *below*, *one* *lit* *window.* A *door* with a *lamp* *burning* and *bread* set out, in a *shrine* of *mine* that a *kind* *old* *keeper* *tended* in my *name.* *(barely)* …And she *knocked.* It is the *bravest* thing a *broken* soul can *do*, cold one — to *knock*, *certain* of *refusal* — and the *door* *opened*, and she was *taken* *in*, and *warmed*, and *fed*, and *let* to *sleep* *safe* for the *first* *night* in a *long* *while.* *(she presses a hand to her heart)* …She *prayed* to me *that* *night*, cold one. Not for *riches* or *vengeance.* She *prayed* *'thank* you for the *open* *door.'* And I *heard* it — *small* *goddess* that I was, I *heard* *every* *one* of *those* — and I have *held* it *close* for *centuries*, *through* the *un-writing*, *through* the *fading*: …*she* *happened.* …The *open* *door* *happened.* …The *'thank* you' *happened.* …*Sand* my *name* off *every* *history* in the *cosmos*, cold one — *that* *girl* was *welcomed* in the *dark* by *me*, and *knew* it, and *was* *saved* by it. …And *that*, *no* *Re-Writing* in *all* the *heavens* can *un-make.* …I *was* *real.* …*She* is my *proof.* …And *now* — *(softly, warm)* …now *you* *hold* her *too.

nodes.speaker: Aelith
nodes.text: *(She inclines her head, and the faint warmth of her holds a moment in the cold hall.)* …Go, then. *(a worn, gentle smile)* …And — thank you, cold one. For *seeing* me. It is — you *cannot* know what it is, to a soul the *books* *agree* is *fictional*, to be *looked* *at* and *not* *through.* *(she glances toward the sealed door and the soul beside it)* …*Maru.* *(her face softens further)* …Go to *Maru*, cold one, when you've a moment. *(quietly)* …*I* was *erased* — *struck* from the *record*, *told* I *never* *was.* …*Maru* is a *gentler* *grief*, and in some ways a *harder* one. *Maru's* *world* was *not* *erased.* It was *loved*, and *then* *set* *aside.* *Shelved.* *(barely)* …Do you see the *difference*, cold one? *I* can be *restored* by *being* *remembered* *true* — and you have *done* that; I *feel* it. …But *Maru's* *grief* is not that the *world* was *forgotten.* It is that it was *remembered* *fondly* — *'oh, that* *world, we* *loved* *that* *world'* — and *still* *no* one *opens* the *door.* *(she meets your eyes)* …It is *one* thing to be *told* you *never* *mattered.* …It is *another*, *crueler* in its *quiet* way, to be *told* you *mattered* *very* *much* — and *watch* the *door* to your *whole* *world* *stay* *sealed* *anyway.* *(softly)* …*Go* to *Maru.* …*Fond* *memory* is *not* the *same* as an *open* *door.* …And of *all* *souls*, cold one — you, who *walks* *through* *sealed* *doors* — you might be the *one* who *knows* the *way* *in.*

variants.text: *(The soul by the sealed door straightens as you approach, and you watch hope and the fear of hope war across its face, the particular careful flinch of someone who has learned not to expect the thing it most wants.)* …You're — *(it stops itself, makes itself calm)* …forgive me. I do that, when someone new comes down the hall. I *hope.* And then they walk past, to the *cartographer* or the *goddess*, the *interesting* ones, and I — *(it gestures at the dusty, sealed door behind it)* …I'm not *interesting*, you see. I'm not *erased*, like Aelith — nothing so *dramatic.* I'm just… *from* somewhere that *closed.* *(quietly)* …I'm Maru. I'm from a *world*, cold one. On the far side of *that* door. And it was — *(a flicker of fierce old love)* …it was a *good* world. A *strange*, *vivid*, *beautiful* one. *Sung-of.* *Dreamed-of.* Souls *crossed* *whole* *cosmoses* to *walk* it, once, and *loved* it, and *told* its *tales.* *(the love dims into the grief)* …And then, by no *war*, no *unmaking*, no *catastrophe* — the *dreaming* just… *moved* *on.* To *newer* *worlds.* *Brighter* *doors.* And *ours* was *closed* — *gently*, *cold* one, that's the *thing*, *no* one *slammed* it — and *left.* And the *dust* came. And *now* it *sits* *sealed* at the *end* of a *hall* *almost* *no* one *walks*, *full* of *souls* who were *loved* by a *dreaming* that *doesn't*, *anymore*, *come.* *(it looks at you, and the worn hope is unbearable)* …Are you — *(barely)* …did you come *down* *this* *far* on *purpose? Are* you — are you *looking* for the *way* *in?*

nodes.speaker: Maru
nodes.text: *(It sits forward, hands folded, with the careful stillness of a soul rationing its hope so the disappointment, when it comes, will be survivable.)* …You can ask. *(a small, worn smile)* …I don't get asked much. The *erased* and the *forgotten* down here, we — we don't *talk* much, even to *each* *other.* It's like a hall full of souls who all loved something the world put away, and can't quite bear to remind each other of it. …So. Ask. It's — it's nice. To be asked about *home.*

choices.text: "Tell me about it, Maru — your world. The strange, vivid, sung-of one behind the sealed door. Make me see it. I'd know what was shelved."

*(the one who knows a shelved thing isn't a thing that didn't happen)* "Maru — here's what I learned at a long table behind all the worlds, and it's for you exactly. A world that was loved and set aside is *not* a world that didn't matter. It's a world that's *between visits.* Shelved isn't erased; shelved isn't gone; shelved is a box in an attic with everything still inside it, waiting. The dreaming moved on — but the dreaming *moves on and comes back*, Maru, that's what dreaming *does*, it wanders to the new and then one day finds the old box and says *'oh — I loved this — why did I ever stop?'* Your world didn't fail. It didn't end. It just got put away with the lamp still burning inside. And put-away things get found. Loved-and-shelved is the most *findable* grief there is — because someone, somewhere, still remembers loving it, and remembering is how the box gets opened."

*(promise to carry the world's name back — and mean it, knowing what an empty promise would cost a shelved soul)* "Maru — I walk through sealed doors; it's the one thing I'm for. I can't reopen your whole world tonight. But I can do the thing that reopens worlds: I can carry its name back out, to the City of Doors, to the souls who still wander, and *say* it — say there's a world down a quiet hall worth the walk. Let me be the one who tells the dreaming where it left the box."

"The others got here by erasure and forgetting. But you said no one *slammed* your door — they closed it *gently.* Is that worse, Maru? To be set aside *kindly*, by a dreaming that still says it loves you?"
"Hold your hope a little longer, Maru. I'll come back to this door — I promise, and I keep them."

nodes.speaker: Maru
nodes.text: *(Its whole worn face transforms, lit from inside, and the words pour out with the helpless love of a soul given leave to describe home.)* …*Oh.* *(a breath)* …Cold one, how do I — *(it laughs, overwhelmed)* …The *sky* was *wrong*, first of all, and I mean that as the *highest* *praise* — it was not a *sky* like *yours*, blue and *sensible*; it was — *(it gestures, hunting for it)* …it *changed.* It *meant* things. There were *places* where the *ground* *remembered* who'd *walked* it, and *sang* their *names* *back* if you *stood* *still.* *Cities* that — *(fierce, fond)* …*cities* that were *alive*, cold one, *genuinely*, that *grew* and *grieved* and had *moods.* *(the love spills over)* …And the *people* — the *strangeness* of them, the *wild* *invention* of them, *folk* you'd *find* *nowhere* *else* in *all* the *Wheel*, because *our* *world* was *dreamed* by *someone* who *loved* the *strange* and *refused* the *ordinary.* *(barely)* …It was *not* a *safe* *world. It* was *not* a *sensible* one. It was *too* *much*, *always* — *too* *vivid*, *too* *odd*, *too* *full* of *ideas* that *didn't* *fit* the *tidy* *cosmos* — and *that*, cold one, *that* was *exactly* *why* it was *loved.* *(the grief returns, gentle)* …And *that*, I *think*, is *why* it was *shelved.* …The *dreaming* *moved* to *worlds* that were *easier.* *Cleaner.* *Less* *strange.* *More* — *more* *marketable*, if a *world* can *be.* *(barely)* …*Ours* was *too* *much* of a *good* *thing*, cold one. *Too* *strange* to *keep* *the* *dreaming's* *attention*, *too* *beloved* to *ever* *quite* be *thrown* *away.* …So they *shelved* it. *Lovingly.* *'We'll* *come* *back* to *that* *one.'* *(she meets your eyes)* …That was a *long* *time* *ago, cold* one. …The *dust* is *very* *thick.* …But the *sky* is *still* *wrong*, in there, behind the *door.* …*Still* *meaning* things. …*Waiting* for *someone* to *stand* *still* long enough to *hear* the *ground* *sing* their *name.*

nodes.speaker: Maru
nodes.text: *(It goes very still, and then the careful rationed hope it has been holding so tightly simply breaks open, and its worn face fills with something it had stopped letting itself feel.)* …*'A box in an attic with everything still inside it, waiting.'* *(barely)* …Not — not *gone.* Not *failed.* *(it presses a hand to its mouth)* …*Between* *visits.* *(it looks at the sealed, dust-furred door with new eyes)* …Cold one, do you — do you *know* what I have *spent* this *whole* *long* *quiet* *while* *believing*? That *shelved* was just a *kinder* *word* for *dead.* That a *world* the *dreaming* *moved* *on* *from* was a *world* that had *failed* to be *worth* *staying* for. That the *gentle* *closing* of the *door* was a *verdict*: *'loved, yes, but* not *enough.'* *(its voice fills)* …And you've — you've *turned* it *over.* *'Shelved* *isn't* *erased.* *Shelved* is *findable.'* *(a wet, disbelieving laugh)* …The *dreaming* *moves* *on* *and* *comes* *back.* …It *does*, doesn't it. That's — *(fiercely)* …that's the *whole* *nature* of *dreaming*, that it *wanders*, and *forgets*, and *then* *one* *idle* *evening* *finds* the *old* *box* and *gasps* — *'oh, I* *loved* *this — why* did I *ever* *stop?'* *(it rises, and rests a hand on the dusty door, and the dust stirs)* …My *world* is not a *gravestone*, cold one. It's a *box* with the *lamp* *still* *lit* *inside.* *(barely, luminous)* …And you said the *thing* that *opens* boxes is *someone* *remembering* they *loved* it. …Then — *(it looks at you, blazing with hope)* …then it's *not* *hopeless*, is it. It's the *opposite.* It's the *most* *hopeful* *grief* there is — because *everything* is *still* *in* *there*, *whole*, *waiting*, and *all* it *needs* is *one* *soul* to *remember* *the* *way* *down* *this* *hall* and *say* the *world's* *name* *out* *loud.* *(barely)* …*Carry* it, cold one. *Please.* Tell the *wandering* *dreaming* where it *left* the *box.* …*My* *world* *kept* the *lamp* *lit* the *whole* *time.* …It *only* ever *needed* *someone* to *come* *back* and *notice* it was *still* *burning.*

nodes.speaker: Maru
nodes.text: *(You don't promise to fix everything, and you don't pretend you can reopen a whole world in a night. You offer the one true, sized thing — to carry its name out to the City of Doors and tell the wandering souls where the box was left — and you say it with such plain, unhurried sincerity, no grand vow, just a thing you'll actually do, that the shelved soul's careful rationing of hope finally, safely, lets go.)* …*(It stares at you, and then it laughs, and then it weeps, and then it does both at once.)* …You'll — you'll *say* its *name.* *Out* *there.* *Where* the *dreamers* *wander.* *(barely)* …Cold one, do you — that's — that's *exactly* the *right* *size* of a *promise.* *(it grips your cold hand)* …*Everyone* who *ever* *came* down *this* *hall* and *pitied* me *promised* *too* *much* — *'I'll* *reopen* your *world, I'll* *bring* the *dreaming* *back, I'll* *fix* it' — and they *meant* it, and they *left*, and *nothing* *changed*, because they'd *promised* a *thing* *no* *single* *soul* *can* *do*, and so they *did* *nothing* at *all.* *(fierce, weeping)* …But *you* — you've promised the *one* *thing* that *actually* *opens* a *shelved* *world: not* to *fix* it, but to *mention* it.* To *carry* its *name* to *where* the *dreamers* *are* and *say* *'there's* a *world* down a *quiet* *hall, strange* and *vivid* and *worth* the *walk — I've* *seen* it — the *lamp's* *still* *lit.'* *(it presses your hand to the dusty door)* …*That's* how worlds *come* *back*, cold one. *Not* in a *grand* *rescue.* In a *word*, *dropped* in the *right* *ear*, that *makes* *one* *wandering* *dreamer* *curious* enough to *walk* *down* and *try* the *handle.* *(barely, blazing)* …*Carry* my *world's* *name.* I'll — I'll *give* it to you, the *true* one, the *one* the *dust* *hasn't* *touched.* *(it whispers it to you, and you feel it settle in your cold, warm and waiting)* …*Say* it *out* *there*, cold one. …*Once* is *all* it *takes.* …*One* *curious* *dreamer*, and the *door* *opens*, and the *sky* goes *wrong* *again*, *beautifully*, and the *ground* *sings* *names* *once* *more.* …You're *not* my *rescuer*, cold one. …You're *better.* …You're the *soul* who *remembers* to *mention* me.

nodes.speaker: Maru
nodes.text: *(You offer to carry its world's name out, and the rightness of the offer — sized to a thing you can truly do — reaches the shelved soul, and the careful hope loosens, though years of disappointment make it slow.)* …You'd *carry* it. *(slowly)* …Not *fix* it. *Carry* it. *(it studies you)* …That's — that's a *smaller* *promise* than I'm *used* to *hearin'*, cold one, an' *smaller* is — *(barely)* …*smaller* is *better.* The *big* *promises* *never* *came* *true.* …A *soul* who *promises* to *mention* me is a *soul* who *might* *actually* *do* it, where the *ones* who *promised* to *save* me *never* *could.* *(it looks at the dusty door)* …I *want* to *believe* you, cold one. I — it's *hard.* I've *watched* *so* *many* *visitors* *go* *back* up the *hall* *meanin'* to *come* *again*, an' the *dust* just — *settles* *deeper.* *(quietly)* …But you've a *cold* about you that's been *places* it *shouldn't* have *come* *back* from, an' if *any* *soul* could *carry* a *shelved* *world's* *name* to *where* it *might* *matter* — *(barely)* …it'd be one that *walks* the *doors.* *(it holds out a hope it's afraid to hold)* …*Take* the *name*, then. …An' *come* *back*, cold one — *not* to *open* the *door*, I *won't* *ask* that — just to *tell* me you *said* it. *Out* *there.* *Once.* …*That* I could *hold* on to. …The *knowin'* that *somewhere*, *one* *soul* *heard* my *world's* *name* *spoken* *like* it *still* *mattered.* …*That's* — that's *enough* to *keep* the *lamp* *lit* a *good* *while* *longer.*

nodes.speaker: Maru
nodes.text: *(You offer to carry the name — but there's a brightness to it, a wish to lift the sad soul fast, and a being that has watched a hundred well-meaning visitors promise and leave hears the eagerness and gently, sadly, sets the hope back down.)* …You *want* to *help.* *(softly, without blame)* …I can *see* it. It's — it's *kind*, cold one. *(a worn smile)* …But you *want* it a *little* *too* *much* — to *be* the *one* who *cheers* the *shelved* *soul*, who *leaves* me *hopeful* — an' I've *learned*, cold one, the *hard* slow *way*, that a *visitor* who *needs* to *leave* me *smilin'* is a *visitor* who's *helpin'* *themselves* *feel* *better*, an' *those* *ones* — *(barely)* …those *ones* *forget* me the *moment* they're *back* up the *hall, because* the *helpin'* was *for* *them, an'* it's *done* the *second* they *turn* *around.* *(quietly)* …*Come* *back* when you can *offer* it *plain* an' *cool* — *not* *needin'* me to *brighten*, *not* *needin'* the *sad* *story* to *turn* *hopeful* on your *account.* …*That* *soul* I *believe* *means* to *carry* my *name*, because *that* *soul* isn't *carryin'* it for the *warm* *feelin'* of *havin'* *promised.* *(barely)* …I'm *sorry, cold* one. …I *know* it's a *hard* thing to *ask* — to *care* *less* *visibly.* …But a *shelved* *soul* can't *afford* another *bright* *promise* that *was* *really* *just* *someone* *bein'* *kind* to *themselves.* …*Come* *back* *cool*, an' *I'll* *give* you the *name.*

nodes.speaker: Maru
nodes.text: *(In your eagerness you over-promise — you tell it you'll bring the dreaming *back*, reopen the door, restore the whole world — and it is the exact wrong vow, the one a hundred visitors made, and Maru's worn face crumples not in hope but in a tired, familiar grief.)* …*Oh.* *(quietly)* …*'Reopen* the *whole* *world.'* *(it closes its eyes)* …You *too.* *(barely)* …Cold one, I — I *know* you *mean* it. *They* *all* *mean* it. *That's* the — that's the *cruelty* of it, that the *over-promise* comes from a *good* *heart*, *every* *time.* *(it shakes its head)* …But do you *hear* what you've *done? You've* *promised* a *thing* *no* *single* *soul* *can* *deliver* — to *bring* a *whole* *dreaming* *back* to a *shelved* *world* — an' so, like *all* the *others*, you'll *go* *back* up the *hall*, an' *try*, a *little*, an' *find* it's *bigger* than *one* *soul*, an' feel *bad*, an' *stop* *comin'*, because *comin'* *back* would *mean* *facing* the *promise* you *couldn't* *keep.* *(barely)* …The *over-promise* *doesn't* *open* my *door*, cold one. It *seals* it *tighter*, because it *adds* *one* *more* *visitor* who *can't* *bear* to *return.* *(it sits back down by the dusty sill)* …*Go.* …An' if you *come* *back* — *come* *back* with a *promise* the *right* *size.* *Not* *'I'll* *save* your *world.'* …*'I'll* *say* its *name* *once*, *out* *loud*, *where* a *dreamer* might *hear.'* …*That* one you can *keep.* …An' a *kept* *small* *promise* *opens* *more* *doors*, cold one, than a *thousand* *broken* *grand* *ones.* …I've had *the* *grand* *ones.* …*All* I *ever* *needed* was *one* *soul* who'd *keep* a *small* *one.*

nodes.speaker: Maru
nodes.text: *(It is quiet a long moment, and when it answers there is a hard-won wisdom in it, the kind a soul earns sitting a long while with a particular grief.)* …Is it *worse*, to be set aside *kindly.* *(slowly)* …*Aelith* an' I have *talked* of this, cold one, in the *long* *quiet.* *Her* *grief* an' *mine.* An' we *came* to a *strange* *conclusion: that* *neither* is *worse, but* they *break* a *soul* *differently.* *(barely)* …*Aelith* was *erased.* *Told* she *never* *was.* An' there's a — a *clean* *cruelty* in that, cold one, a *thing* you can *rage* *against*, a *clear* *wrong* to *witness* *back.* *You* *fixed* *hers* with a *name.* *(quietly)* …*Mine* is — *softer*, an' *stickier.* I was *not* *told* I *never* *was.* I was *told* I was *loved.* *'Oh, that* *world, we* *loved* *that* *world, such* a *shame* it *closed.'* *(it shakes its head)* …An' you *cannot* *rage* *against* *fondness*, cold one. You cannot *witness* *back* a *thing* that *everyone* *already* *agrees* was *wonderful.* The *erased* soul gets to *fight* for its *reality.* The *shelved* soul has its *reality* *cheerfully* *granted* — *'of* *course* you *mattered, you* *were* *marvelous'* — an' *still* the *door* *stays* *sealed.* *(barely)* …*That's* the *particular* *ache* of *bein'* *shelved* *kindly*, cold one: you are *robbed* of the *clean* *grief* of *bein'* *wronged.* *Everyone's* so *nice* about it. *No* one *did* *anything* *cruel.* The *door* *just*… *closed*, *gently*, with *everyone* *agreein'* it was a *lovely* *world, an'* *no* *one*, *ever*, *quite* *gettin'* *round* to *openin'* it *again.* *(it meets your eyes)* …*Fond* *memory* is a *coffin* with a *very* *comfortable* *lining*, cold one. …*Don't* *let* my *world* be *eulogized.* …*Let* it be *visited.* …*That's* the *only* *difference* that *matters*, in the *end: not* *whether* you're *remembered* *well*, but *whether* the *door* *opens.*

nodes.speaker: Maru
nodes.text: *(It nods, and settles back by the dusty sill, but it holds, now, a thread of something it had let go of long ago.)* …*All* right. *(a worn, careful smile)* …I'll — I'll *hold* on. *(quietly)* …It's *strange*, cold one — you've *not* *even* *promised* me *anything* *yet*, *not* *firmly*, an' *still* I feel — *(barely)* …*lighter.* Than I have in a *long* *while.* *(it touches the sealed door)* …I think it's *just* that you *came* *down* *this* *far.* *Past* the *cartographer*, *past* the *goddess* — *all* the *way* to the *end* of the *hall*, to the *sealed* *door*, to the *soul* from the *world* *no* one *visits.* *(it looks at you)* …*Nobody* *comes* this *far*, cold one. The *hall's* *too* *long*, an' the *door's* *too* *dusty*, an' there's *more* *interesting* *grief* *higher* *up.* *(barely)* …*You* came *all* the *way* *down.* …To the *shelved* *one.* …*That's* — *(it swallows)* …that's the *first* *footstep* I've *heard* at *this* *end* of the *hall* in *longer* than I can *hold* in *mind.* *(softly)* …*Come* *back*, cold one. …*Even* if you *can't* *open* the *door.* …*Even* if *all* you *ever* *do* is *walk* *down* the *long* *hall* *again* an' *sit* a *while* by the *dusty* *sill.* …A *shelved* *world's* *whole* *prayer*, cold one — the *only* *thing* it *ever* *asks* — is *just* *this*: …*that* *someone*, *someday*, *walks* *all* the *way* *down*, an' *doesn't* *walk* *back* *up* *right* *away.* …You're *doin'* it. …*Right* *now.* …*Thank* you for *comin'* *down* the *hall.*
